hooks.command: CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then STAGED=$(git diff --name-only --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$STAGED" | grep -qE '^(agents|runtime|interfaces)/'; then if ! echo "$STAGED" | grep -q '^tests/'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Behavior files in agents/, runtime/, or interfaces/ are staged with no test files. Add or update tests for the changed behavior before committing. Run: npm test"}}'; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then DIFF=$(git diff --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+.*(export (async )?function is[A-Z][a-zA-Z]+\()' && ! echo "$DIFF" | grep -q 'TRIGGER-JUSTIFIED:'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[CLASSIFIER GATE] A new is[X]() classifier function is being committed without TRIGGER-JUSTIFIED comment. Principle 7: checks must run on EVERY message. Add // TRIGGER-JUSTIFIED: [reason] to bypass."}}'; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then DIFF=$(git diff --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+.*(export (async )?function run[A-Z][a-zA-Z]+Agent\()'; then if ! echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+.*(ReadinessNotice|ALWAYS-ON-AUDIT-JUSTIFIED)'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[NEW AGENT GATE] A new run[X]Agent() function is being committed without an always-on platform audit block. Principle 7: every agent must have a platform-injected completeness audit that fires on every message. Options: (1) Add the always-on audit block in message.ts following the designReadinessNotice/archReadinessNotice pattern. (2) If genuinely not applicable, add // ALWAYS-ON-AUDIT-JUSTIFIED: [reason] in the diff and get explicit user approval."}}'; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then STAGED=$(git diff --name-only --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$STAGED" | grep -qFx 'interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts' || echo "$STAGED" | grep -qFx 'runtime/agent-router.ts'; then if ! echo "$STAGED" | grep -qF 'workflows.test.ts'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[E2E SCENARIO GATE] message.ts or agent-router.ts changed without a workflows.test.ts update. Every new path through these files requires a new integration scenario in the same commit -- 100% E2E scenario coverage is a hard invariant. Add the scenario then re-commit."}}'; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then DIFF=$(git diff --cached -- ':(exclude).claude/settings.json' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+.*[A-Z_]{3,}PATTERN\s*=' || echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+.*\.test\(captured[A-Za-z]' || echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+.*\.test\(agent[A-Za-z][a-zA-Z]*Response'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[ENFORCEMENT OUTPUT CONTRACT GATE] Staged code pattern-matches agent output text to detect bad behavior (e.g. DEFERRAL_PATTERN, .test(capturedResponse)). This is always incomplete — there are infinite phrasings of non-compliance. Required fix (Principle 8a): define the output contract first. What must be PRESENT in correct output? Verify presence instead. Example: countRecommendations(response) < requiredCount instead of PATTERN.test(response). Remove the pattern-match gate and implement a structural count or format-presence check."}}'; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git push'; then PENDING=$(npx tsx scripts/mt.ts count 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "$PENDING" -gt 0 ]; then echo "{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"PreToolUse\",\"permissionDecision\":\"deny\",\"permissionDecisionReason\":\"[MT PENDING BLOCK] $PENDING manual test(s) pending in MANUAL_TESTS_PENDING.md. The user must run each one in real Slack and mark it done before this push can proceed. Run: npx tsx scripts/mt.ts list (to see them) | npx tsx scripts/mt.ts next (to start the first one) | npx tsx scripts/mt.ts done MT-N (to mark a test done after PASS). Repeat until 0 pending, then re-run git push. If a test cannot be run yet, explicitly remove it from MANUAL_TESTS_PENDING.md with a backlog entry naming when it will be run.\"}}"; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git push'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[PRE-PUSH READINESS CHECK] Complete these before pushing:
1. DOC ACCURACY: Re-read every doc updated this session (AGENTS.md, SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md, BACKLOG.md). Confirm descriptions match actual implementation, not the plan. Stale docs are a user-visible bug.
2. USER CAN TEST: List what the user needs (env vars, server state, feature state). Surface any missing pieces.
3. GAPS TO BACKLOG: Any gap or shortcut identified this session that has no BACKLOG.md entry must be added now.
4. PRINCIPLE 8 SELF-RATING: If agents/*.ts changed, enumerate: (a) platform-enforced behaviors and where enforcement code lives, (b) prompt-dependent behaviors with justification for why platform enforcement is not possible.
5. ENFORCEMENT OUTPUT CONTRACT: Any enforcement code added to message.ts must verify PRESENCE (count, format marker) — NOT absence of bad patterns. No regex PATTERN constants or pattern-match calls against captured agent output in staged diff.
6. PRIOR DECISIONS: Review every change in this session. If ANY change reverses or modifies a previously agreed format, label, category, UX pattern, or behavior — confirm the user explicitly approved the reversal with a concrete new reason that invalidates the original. Existing decisions are load-bearing until proven otherwise.
7. READ THE OUTPUT: For every user-facing change, paste or describe the actual Slack/UI output you observed during testing. Infrastructure passing (gates fire, logs correct, tests green) is NOT the same as output being correct. Double welcome, empty content blocks, wrong labels, stale text — these are only visible by reading what the user sees. If you did not test user-facing output, say so explicitly.
After completing all six checks, run git push again."}}'; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then STAGED=$(git diff --name-only --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$STAGED" | grep -qFx 'interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts'; then FILE='interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts'; AGENTS=$(grep -cE 'async function run[A-Z][a-zA-Z]+Agent' "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); GATES=$(grep -c 'UPSTREAM-GATE:' "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); EXPECTED=$((AGENTS - 1)); if [ "$GATES" -lt "$EXPECTED" ]; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[UPSTREAM GATE COVERAGE] message.ts has fewer UPSTREAM-GATE markers than expected. Every non-PM agent path must have a // UPSTREAM-GATE: [agent] marker at its pre-run upstream audit. Add the missing marker and gate implementation."}}'; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then DIFF=$(git diff --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+.*(context|inject|domain|boundary|pipeline|feature.*status)' && (git diff --name-only --cached | grep -qE 'agents/|handlers/general\.ts'); then if ! echo "$DIFF" | grep -q 'DESIGN-REVIEWED:'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[DESIGN REVIEW GATE] This commit adds context injection, domain boundary, or cross-agent behavior to agents/ or general.ts without a DESIGN-REVIEWED comment. Principle 12 requires answering: (1) How does this scale to 100 features/10 agents? (2) Which component already owns this? (3) Is this cross-cutting? Add // DESIGN-REVIEWED: [rationale] at the change site."}}'; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then STAGED=$(git diff --name-only --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$STAGED" | grep -qFx 'interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts'; then FILE='interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts'; REPLY_PATHS=$(grep -c 'branch=.*reply\|branch=.*revision-reply' "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); WRITEBACKS=$(grep -c 'patchProductSpecWithRecommendations\|patchEngineeringSpecWithDecision' "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "$WRITEBACKS" -lt "$REPLY_PATHS" ]; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[ESCALATION WRITEBACK GATE] message.ts has fewer spec writeback calls than escalation reply paths. Every escalation confirmation path must write back to the upstream spec. Check that all branch=*-reply paths have patchProductSpecWithRecommendations or patchEngineeringSpecWithDecision."}}'; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then UNSTAGED=$(git diff --name-only 2>/dev/null | grep -vE '^coverage/|^\.conversation|^\.confirmed' | head -5); if [ -n "$UNSTAGED" ]; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[COMMIT EVERYTHING] There are unstaged tracked files. Stage all changed files before committing — no selective staging. Unstaged files: '"$(echo $UNSTAGED | tr '\n' ', ')"'"}}'; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then STAGED=$(git diff --name-only --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$STAGED" | grep -qE '^interfaces/slack/handlers/'; then DIFF=$(git diff --cached -- 'interfaces/slack/handlers/' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+[^+].*console\.log\([\"\x60]\\?\[[A-Z]'; then if ! echo "$STAGED" | grep -qFx 'runtime/boot-fingerprint.ts'; then if ! echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE 'MARKER-BUMP-JUSTIFIED:'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[CODE_MARKER BUMP GATE — Block J3] Staged diff adds new bracketed console.log lines in interfaces/slack/handlers/ (e.g. [HEDGE-GATE], [READINESS]) but runtime/boot-fingerprint.ts is not staged. The CODE_MARKER is the contract between the fix author and the manual tester: if you ship a new production log line without bumping the marker, manual testers cannot prove they are running the new code (the tsx-watch silent-reload-failure footgun observed 2026-04-29). Either: (1) bump CODE_MARKER in runtime/boot-fingerprint.ts and stage it, OR (2) add // MARKER-BUMP-JUSTIFIED: [reason] in the diff if this log line does not need production verification."}}'; fi; fi; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then STAGED=$(git diff --name-only --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$STAGED" | grep -qE '^(interfaces/slack/(app|server|handlers/(message|commands|thinking))\.ts|runtime/(agents/|conversation-store|boot-fingerprint|routing/(spec-parser|snapshot|migrate-routing-state))\.ts)'; then if ! echo "$STAGED" | grep -qFx 'MANUAL_TESTS.md'; then DIFF=$(git diff --cached 2>/dev/null); if ! echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE 'MT-CHECKED:'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[MANUAL TEST GATE — Block J2] Staged diff modifies a file covered by MT-N scenarios in MANUAL_TESTS.md (interfaces/slack/handlers, runtime/agents, conversation-store, routing entry-points, boot-fingerprint). The fix author must explicitly acknowledge which manual scenario(s) need re-running. Either: (1) update MANUAL_TESTS.md (add a new scenario, update the Last verified line for the relevant entry, or note the change), OR (2) add // MT-CHECKED: [MT-N or none-applicable, with reason] in the diff if no manual re-test is needed."}}'; fi; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then DIFF=$(git diff --cached -- ':(exclude)tests/fixtures/' ':(exclude).claude/settings.json' ':(exclude).claude/settings.local.json' ':(exclude)tests/invariants/secrets-hygiene.test.ts' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$DIFF" | grep -E '^\+' | grep -v 'SECRET-FALSE-POSITIVE:' | grep -qE '(xox[abps]-[0-9]{10,}-[0-9]{10,}|sk-ant-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{32,}|sk-proj-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{32,}|gh[psoaur]_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36,}|AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|-----BEGIN ((RSA|DSA|EC|OPENSSH|PGP) )?PRIVATE KEY-----)'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[SECRETS HYGIENE GATE — Block L3] Staged diff contains a high-confidence secret pattern (Slack token, Anthropic/OpenAI key, GitHub PAT, AWS access key, or private-key block). Required action: (1) ROTATE the credential immediately if it is real — assume it is compromised the moment it touches a diff. (2) Remove the value from the staged change. (3) If the value is a documented example or test fixture, add // SECRET-FALSE-POSITIVE: <reason> on the same line. Allowed paths (skipped by this gate): tests/fixtures/, .claude/settings*.json, the scanner itself."}}'; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit -m'; then STAGED=$(git diff --name-only --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$STAGED" | grep -qE '^(interfaces/slack/(app|server|handlers/(message|commands|thinking))\.ts|runtime/(agents/|claude-client|conversation-store|deterministic-auditor|brand-auditor|action-verifier|escalation-orchestrator|orientation-enforcer|phase-completion-auditor|spec-auditor|tool-handlers|html-renderer|routing/(spec-parser|snapshot|migrate-routing-state))\.ts|agents/[a-z]+\.ts)'; then MSG=$(echo "$CMD" | sed -n 's/.*-m "\(.*\)".*/\1/p' | head -c 4000); if ! echo "$MSG" | grep -qE 'MT-NEEDED:|MT-NONE:'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[MT NEEDED GATE] Commit modifies a runtime-behavior path (handlers, runtime/agents, runtime/claude-client, runtime/conversation-store, runtime/*-auditor, agents/*) but the commit message does not declare which manual scenarios are required. Add ONE of:
  MT-NEEDED: MT-7, MT-16   (lists the MTs the user must run; you must also append to MANUAL_TESTS_PENDING.md in the same commit)
  MT-NONE: <reason>          (declares no manual test applies — be specific)
Without this declaration, manual-test debt accumulates silently."}}'; fi; fi; fi; true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit'; then DIFF=$(git diff --cached 2>/dev/null); if echo "$DIFF" | grep -qE '^\+.*(userMessage|rawUserMessage)\.(includes|startsWith|match|indexOf)\(' && ! echo "$DIFF" | grep -q 'KEYWORD-JUSTIFIED:'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"[FREE TEXT CLASSIFICATION] Code uses keyword/substring matching on user message content (includes, startsWith, match, indexOf). Human free-text must be classified by LLM (Haiku), not keywords. Add // KEYWORD-JUSTIFIED: [reason] if this is matching machine-generated structured input, not user prose."}}'; fi; fi; true

FILE=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""' 2>/dev/null); echo "$FILE" | grep -q '\.html$' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"HTML files must be generated by renderFromSpec() in runtime/html-renderer.ts -- never hand-written. Call the function and write the result via fs.writeFileSync() in a script."}}' || true

FILE=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""' 2>/dev/null); echo "$FILE" | grep -q 'agentic-health360' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"agentic-health360 is a read-only customer repo. Fix the platform parser instead of modifying the customer repo. See Principle 3a in CLAUDE.md."}}' || true
python3 /Users/ssahgal/Developer/agentic-sdlc/scripts/hooks/check-hardcoding.py 2>/dev/null || true
python3 /Users/ssahgal/Developer/agentic-sdlc/scripts/hooks/check-plan.py 2>/dev/null || true

python3 -c "
import sys, json, subprocess, os, datetime, re
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
msg = data.get('message', '')
is_slack = bool(re.search(r'\\[[0-9]+:[0-9]+ [AP]M\\]|Health360SDLC|UX Designer|Product Manager', msg))
if is_slack:
    log_file = '/Users/ssahgal/Developer/agentic-sdlc/logs/bot-' + datetime.date.today().isoformat() + '.log'
    if os.path.exists(log_file):
        result = subprocess.run(['tail', '-150', log_file], capture_output=True, text=True)
        ctx = '[AUTO LOG CONTEXT] Slack paste detected — bot logs (last 150 lines):\\n' + result.stdout
        print(json.dumps({'hookSpecificOutput': {'hookEventName': 'UserPromptSubmit', 'additionalContext': ctx}}))
    else:
        print(json.dumps({'hookSpecificOutput': {'hookEventName': 'UserPromptSubmit', 'additionalContext': '[AUTO LOG CONTEXT] Slack paste detected but no log file for today: ' + log_file}}))
" 2>/dev/null || true

file=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$file" | grep -q 'DECISIONS.md'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookSpecificOutput": "PostToolUse", "additionalContext": "[BACKLOG ENFORCEMENT] You just wrote to DECISIONS.md. For every gap or shortcut added or modified: (1) Is there already a backlog item for it? If not, add one now in the correct ordered position before Step 5. (2) The backlog item must include: the problem today, what changes, why deferred, and source reference to DECISIONS.md. DECISIONS.md entries without a corresponding BACKLOG.md item are incomplete."}}'; fi

file=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$file" | grep -qE '/(agents|runtime|interfaces)/[^/]+\.ts$'; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookSpecificOutput": "PostToolUse", "additionalContext": "[CLAUDE.md Definition of Done] Modified agent/runtime/interface file. Confirm ALL that apply:
- AGENTS.md updated (agent persona/capabilities changed)
- SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md updated (data flow or behavior changed)
- BACKLOG.md updated (completed item moved AND any new gaps discovered added)
- DECISIONS.md updated (if any solo-team shortcut taken)
- .env.example updated (if new WorkspaceConfig field added)"}}'; fi

file=$(jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$file" | grep -q "/memory/feedback_"; then echo "{\"hookSpecificOutput\": {\"hookSpecificOutput\": \"PostToolUse\", \"additionalContext\": \"[MEMORY vs PRINCIPLE GATE] You just wrote a feedback memory. STOP and answer: Does this rule need to be enforced 100% of the time across all conversations? If YES it belongs in CLAUDE.md as a numbered principle with a hook in .claude/settings.json, NOT in memory. Memory is recalled probabilistically. Principles are enforced structurally. If you already agreed with the user on this rule before and are writing it to memory again, that proves memory failed -- escalate to CLAUDE.md immediately.\"}}"; fi

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookSpecificOutput": "Stop", "additionalContext": "[COVERAGE AUDIT] Before ending: if source files were committed this session, verify npx vitest run --coverage shows functions > 83%. Flag any coverage drops."}}'; true

PENDING=$(npx tsx scripts/mt.ts count 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "$PENDING" -gt 0 ]; then LIST=$(npx tsx scripts/mt.ts list 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr '\n' ' '); echo "{\"hookSpecificOutput\": {\"hookSpecificOutput\": \"Stop\", \"additionalContext\": \"[MT PENDING] $PENDING manual test(s) pending: $LIST. Walk the user through them one at a time. Run \`npx tsx scripts/mt.ts next\` to print the first one's full instructions. The pre-push hook will block pushes until MANUAL_TESTS_PENDING.md is empty.\"}}"; fi; true

OUT=$(bash scripts/discipline-audit.sh 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$OUT" ]; then printf '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookSpecificOutput": "Stop", "additionalContext": %s}}\n' "$(printf '%s' "$OUT" | jq -Rs .)"; fi; true
